insights: Orphan detection logic refined to distinguish between write-ahead logs (events.jsonl) and meaningful work logs
Changed from simple presence check (.next().is_some()) to filtered iteration that excludes events.jsonl
Uses iterator chaining with flatten() and any() to filter directory entries while checking file types
Variable renamed from has_logs to has_meaningful_logs for clarity
Two assertion statements reformatted for multi-line readability (no functional changes)
Assertions in test_meta_checksum function now span multiple lines per style guide
Pure formatting improvement, no logic modification

patterns_discovered.description: events.jsonl (write-ahead log) should not be counted as evidence of work product when determining if a card is orphaned. Storage model uses append-only JSONL for transaction logging but this shouldn't prevent cleanup detection.
Using flatten() → any() with filter predicate to iterate directory entries while simultaneously filtering and type-checking in a single expression
Breaking assertions across multiple lines when message exceeds readability threshold, maintaining consistent style for test code clarity

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: events.jsonl presence was false positive for orphan detection
gotchas_discovered.explanation: Previous code counted any log file (including the WAL) as evidence of activity. events.jsonl is created automatically for all cards and should not prevent cleanup of truly orphaned cards.
gotchas_discovered.impact: Would prevent proper cleanup of legitimately orphaned cards that had event history

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Directory iteration requires explicit file type checking
gotchas_discovered.explanation: Not all entries in logs/ directory are files; must call .is_file() to distinguish regular files from directories
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Added && e.path().is_file() check in filter predicate

gotchas_discovered.explanation: Original .next() only checked if ANY entry exists; replaced with .any() to check all entries and apply predicate
gotchas_discovered.improvement: New implementation checks all entries against filter condition

discoveries.approach_outcome: SUCCESS - Orphan detection logic corrected to properly ignore events.jsonl when determining if a card is truly orphaned. The fix aligns with the project's storage decision (events.jsonl as write-ahead log, not work artifact). Code formatting improvements applied for consistency. Make check verification successful.
recommendations: Extract a helper function is_meaningful_log_file(path: &Path) -> bool to centralize the 'exclude events.jsonl' logic and improve readability
Document in code comment what constitutes a 'meaningful log' vs WAL (events.jsonl), referencing storage-decision.md
Consider adding a test case that verifies an orphaned card with only events.jsonl is correctly identified as orphaned
If additional log files should be excluded in future (e.g., other WAL formats), the helper function provides a single point of modification
Review other cleanup/orphan detection code paths to ensure consistent treatment of events.jsonl
